name: SuprSend Multi-Channel Notification API
what_it_does: Multi-channel notification infrastructure API for sending push, email, SMS, WhatsApp, Slack, and in-app notifications through a unified API — with workflow automation, subscriber management, preference centers, and routing logic built in.
use_cases: Sending multi-channel notifications (push + email fallback) through single API call
Building notification workflows with conditional routing across 11+ channels
Managing subscriber preferences and opt-outs programmatically via API
Triggering event-based notification campaigns from agent workflows
Replacing custom notification infrastructure with managed multi-channel service
not_for: Pure transactional email only (simpler solutions like Postmark are sufficient)
Bulk email marketing at Mailchimp scale (SuprSend focuses on notifications)
Teams needing on-premise deployment for data compliance
best_when: An agent needs to send notifications across multiple channels with fallback logic, preference management, and workflow orchestration through a single API.
avoid_when: You only need a single channel (email or SMS only) where simpler providers offer better pricing and simplicity.

auth.notes: Workspace key and secret from SuprSend dashboard. HMAC-SHA256 signature for secure requests. Separate keys per workspace/environment. Webhooks include signature verification.

pricing.notes: Generous free tier for development. Pricing based on notifications sent across all channels. Channel costs vary (SMS more expensive than push). Good pricing for multi-channel vs. building custom infrastructure.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per plan; documented in dashboard

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API and SDKs well-documented with workflow examples. Multi-language SDKs simplify integration. Webhook delivery for notification status events. HMAC signing adds security complexity for agents.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency key support for notification sends prevents duplicate delivery. Subscriber management operations are upsert-style. Workflow triggers are idempotent with same idempotency key.
known_agent_gotchas: HMAC signing required — agents must implement request signing logic
Channel credentials (APNS, FCM, etc.) must be configured before sending push
Workflow templates built in UI — agents cannot create templates via API
Subscriber distinction from user accounts — manage subscriber objects carefully

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HMAC-SHA256 request signing. SOC2 Type II certified. GDPR compliant. Webhook signature verification. US and EU data residency options available.
